methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $73M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
LabGenius is a Series B-stage biotech company founded by James Field that has raised $73M to date. Valuation analysis relies on a comparison universe of 5 private peers in the same sector and stage (Eikon Therapeutics, Neko Health, New Limit, Asimov, Profluent) alongside 3 public biotech comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, anchoring to the $73M raised and implying a value of $139M. Peer-median analysis draws on the five same-sector, same-stage private companies, which exhibit a median valuation of $810M with a range spanning $600M to $3.3B, directly supporting an $810M estimate. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to absence of revenue data for the target. These two methods triangulate to a valuation range of $118M (low) to $931M (median), with a midpoint of $474M.

The wide dispersion reflects genuine heterogeneity within the Series B biotech cohort rather than methodological weakness. Stage-progression yields the lower bound, reflecting only capital deployed; peer-median anchors to observed market practice but is pulled upward by high-value outliers (Eikon at $3.3B). Key uncertainties center on LabGenius's relative position within its peer set—specifically, progress on clinical or technical milestones relative to comparables—and whether the target should be valued toward the lower (capital-efficient) or upper (high-upside) end of the private peer distribution.
